calendar.matters:
Hearing Re: Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Feb-15-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Willis, Esq. and Margaret Prinsing, Esq. 510-346-6204 of Remcho Johansen & Purcell LLP for Petitioner Noah Shanok.
Joshua White, Deputy City Attorney 415-554-4661 Office of the City Attorney for Respondent John Arntz and City and County of San Francisco.
James Sutton, Esq. 415-732-7700 of the Sutton Law Firm for Real Party in Interest Catherine Stefani.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court commends parties for their additional briefs and announces reasons for not being convinced or persuaded. Petitioner argues. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Hearing Re: Petition For Writ Of Mandate. The Court adopts the tentative ruling denying petition for writ of mandate for reasons stated on the tentative ruling filed on 2/14/18.
(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Counsel for Respondent to prepare a proposed order and attach the Court's tentative ruling.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

Hearing Re: Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Feb-13-2018 Continued To Feb-15-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Willis, Esq. and Margaret Prinsing, Esq. 510-346-6204 of Remcho Johansen & Purcell LLP for Petitioner Noah Shanok.
Joshua White, Deputy City Attorney 415-554-4661 Office of the City Attorney for Respondent John Arntz and City and County of San Francisco.
James Sutton, Esq. 415-732-7700 of the Sutton Law Firm for Real Party in Interest Catherine Stefani.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court distributes copies of the tentative ruling prior to the hearing. Hearing is held. Petitioner Argues voter's intent is the most important.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes)

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Hearing Re: Petition For Writ Of Mandate is continued to 2/15/18, Thursday at 9am in Dept. 302 before Judge Kahn. Parties letter briefing is due by tomorrow, Wednesday, 2/14/18. Briefing to to be submitted directly by email to Judge Kahn. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)
